pattern: Land that touches the border can escape, so flood-fill inward from every boundary land cell to erase the reachable land, and whatever ones remain are the trapped enclave cells.
hints: A land cell can leave the grid exactly when it is connected to the border, so the cells that cannot escape are those never reached from the edge.
Run a flood fill starting from every land cell on the four borders, marking everything connected to them as escapable.
After erasing all border-connected land, count the land cells that survive — those are the enclaves.

edge_inputs.description: three enclosed and one boundary-touching land cell
single land cell is on the border so escapes
interior land bridged to the border by one cell

stress.description: random binary grid where land is denser inside, mixing escapable and trapped regions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 200))
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    grid = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.45 else 0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: moderate dimensions keep the flood fill fast
